methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $539M - $10.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Chai Discovery

data.narrative:
Biobot Analytics is a Series B biotech and health company that has raised $40M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comparison set of 11 peers: 3 public companies (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private entities across Biotech & Health, including same-stage and adjacent-stage companies like Chai Discovery, Eikon Therapeutics, and Colossal Biosciences.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $76M. Peer-Median anchors to the median valuation of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (Colossal Biosciences at $10.3B, Chai Discovery at $3.8B, New Limit at $3.1B, and Eikon Therapeutics at $539M), implying $3.5B. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of revenue disclosure for the target. The sector median P/S across public comps is 6.9x, but lacks applicability without revenue data.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $65M (low) to $4.0B (high), with a median of $1.8B across the two applicable methods. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around revenue trajectory and market positioning relative to the peer set. The stage-progression method provides a conservative floor; peer multiples imply substantial upside conditional on growth execution and clinical/commercial validation typical of biotech stage progression.
